ogTitle: Low Head vs Lower Beulah: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Low Head and Lower Beulah.
heading: Low Head vs Lower Beulah — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Low Head, Tasmania and Lower Beulah, Tasmania. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Lower Beulah has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Low Head (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Low Head or Lower Beulah safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Low Head has a flood risk score of 56/100, while Lower Beulah scores 28/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Low Head scores 62/100 for bushfire risk. Lower Beulah scores 20/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Low Head or Lower Beulah?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Lower Beulah has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.48% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
